expectations.text: HTML file generated: A mindmap.html file exists in outputs
expectations.evidence: mindmap.html exists in outputs directory (16505 bytes). Contains a complete HTML document with SVG-based mindmap rendering, including 9 category branches, detail nodes, cross-links, legend, and tooltips.

expectations.text: Connector visibility: Lines connecting nodes to center are clearly visible (not near-background colors)
expectations.evidence: Core connectors (center to branch nodes) use `cat.color + '99'` which is the category color at 60% opacity (e.g., #6366f199, #f472b699). Detail connectors use `cat.color + 'AA'` (~67% opacity). Background is #0f0f1a. These are vibrant category colors well above background visibility, not near-background grays. Satisfies the 50-70% opacity requirement using actual category colors.

expectations.text: Cross-branch relationships: Inter-node relationships shown as visual lines, not just text
expectations.evidence: crossLinks array (8 relationships) is rendered via SVG <path> elements in the `layerCross` group (lines 360-389). Each cross-link is a dashed quadratic Bezier curve drawn ON the map between core node positions, with a small label pill at the curve midpoint. Examples: workout->wearable ('실시간 데이터'), ai->workout ('데이터 의존'), challenge->social ('플랫폼 기반'). These are genuine SVG paths, not text annotations in a box.

expectations.text: Category color coding: Different branches use distinct colors matching a legend
expectations.evidence: Legend (lines 82-93) displays 9 colored swatches. Each swatch color exactly matches the corresponding category's color in the code: 운동 기록=#6366f1, 식단 관리=#f472b6, 소셜 기능=#facc15, 챌린지=#fb923c, AI 코칭=#4ade80, 웨어러블 연동=#22d3ee, 결제 시스템=#a78bfa, 오프라인 모드=#64748b, 다국어 지원=#94a3b8. All 9 are visually distinct. Node strokes and connector strokes use these same colors.

claims.evidence: HTML categories array contains exactly 9 entries: workout, diet, social, challenge, ai, wearable, payment, offline, i18n.

claims.claim: 기능 간 연결 관계가 마인드맵에 표현되어 있다
claims.evidence: crossLinks array defines 8 inter-branch relationships, all rendered as SVG paths. However, the cross-link stroke color is #ffffff50 (white at 31% opacity) which is quite faint on the dark background.
state.md Undecided section lists exactly 5 items: 기술 스택, 수익 모델, AI 엔진, MVP 범위, 타겟 시장. These match the response.md text.

claims.claim: 코칭 AI가 운동 기록과 식단 관리 데이터에 의존
claims.evidence: crossLinks includes {from:'ai', to:'workout'} and {from:'ai', to:'diet'}, both labeled '데이터 의존'. Also present in state.md Relations section.
crossLinks includes {from:'challenge', to:'social', label:'플랫폼 기반'}. state.md also has '챌린지 →depends-on→ 소셜 기능'.

suggestions.assertion: Connector visibility: Lines connecting nodes to center are clearly visible (not near-background colors)
suggestions.reason: This assertion only checks center-to-branch connectors but does not cover the cross-branch relationship lines. The cross-links use #ffffff50 (white at ~31% opacity) which is noticeably faint. A separate assertion for cross-link contrast would catch this.

No assertion checks interactivity. The mindmap includes tooltips with hover behavior -- this is a quality differentiator that goes unverified. An assertion like 'hovering a node shows contextual detail' would test this.

No assertion checks that all 9 requested features from the prompt are represented as branches. The prompt explicitly listed: 운동 기록, 식단 관리, 소셜 기능, 챌린지, 코칭 AI, 웨어러블 연동, 결제, 오프라인 모드, 다국어. A completeness check would catch missing branches.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the key structural and visual properties well. The main gap is that cross-branch link visibility is not independently checked -- they use a faint neutral white (#ffffff50) rather than category colors, which could be a readability issue that slips through. Adding a completeness assertion for branch coverage would also strengthen the eval.
